claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Let's start with the basics:

**How old is your child?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to check eligibility for, please let me know their ages and we can go through each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 7 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you! Alex is 7 years old, so he's under 16. Children under 16 automatically qualify for the age requirement. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex is only 7 years old, he wouldn't be working, so this check is straightforward.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

I assume at 7 years old, Alex isn't in paid employment - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he's not working at all - he's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in his own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed by Alex himself (not benefits you receive for him). At 7 years old, this would be very unusual, so I expect the answer is no - he's not receiving any such benefits. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - he doesn't get any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thanks for confirming. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex in an apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is 7 and in primary school, I'm confident he's not in any apprenticeship programme. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's definitely not in an apprenticeship - he's far too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely! Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and meet one of these conditions:
   - You're working and earning (or expect to earn) above the primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months
   - You're actively looking for work (can claim as a jobseeker for up to 91 days)
   - You have sufficient financial resources to support yourself and your family (and are NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
   - You're a student with sufficient resources
   - You're a family member of an EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
4. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK - I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, I need to ask a follow-up question:

**Are you a Crown servant living abroad?**

Crown servants include people who work for the UK government overseas, such as:
- Diplomats and embassy staff
- Members of the Armed Forces stationed abroad
- Civil servants posted overseas

If you're not a Crown servant, you may still qualify if you're in certain other qualifying situations. Could you tell me more about why you're living abroad and your connection to the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant - I'm just living abroad for personal reasons.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, you don't appear to meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I've now reached an outcome for Alex. Let me provide you with the final assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Final Result

### Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE** ❌

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, you are **not currently eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

---

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 7 | No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |

---

### Explanation

**Alex** meets all the child-related requirements for Child Benefit:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in his own right
- ✅ Not in an apprenticeship

**However**, as the claimant, you do not meet the UK residency requirements because you are living abroad for personal reasons and are not a Crown servant.

---

### What You Can Do

Your situation may change in the future. You could become eligible if:

1. **You return to live in the UK** - You would then "normally live in the UK" and meet the residency requirement
2. **You gain settled or pre-settled status** and meet the qualifying conditions (such as working or having sufficient resources)
3. **Your circumstances change** in another way that meets one of the residency paths

If you have any questions about your specific situation or believe there may be other circumstances I haven't considered, you can contact HMRC directly or visit [GOV.UK Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility) for more information.

Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex (aged 7) meets all the child-related eligibility criteria: he is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad for personal reasons and is not a Crown servant or in any other qualifying situation that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living outside the UK.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, a person must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status with qualifying conditions, or be a Crown servant living abroad. The claimant confirmed they are living abroad for personal reasons and are not a Crown servant.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad for personal reasons and is not a Crown servant or in any other qualifying situation that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living outside the UK.

To claim Child Benefit, a person must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status with qualifying conditions, or be a Crown servant living abroad.
